title: pinus balfouriana subsp. longaeva
intro: Pinus balfouriana subsp. longaeva, also known as the Bristlecone Pine, is a slow-growing evergreen tree belonging to the Pinaceae family, characterized by its distinctive twisted trunk and long lifespan. The wood and resin of the Bristlecone Pine have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and combat respiratory infections. The health benefits of Pinus balfouriana subsp. longaeva include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the Bristlecone Pine often take the form of infused oils, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions, or ingested as a supplement to support overall well-being.
